description: Lecture on the Tendermint protocol, discussing consensus protocols and the importance of public key infrastructure (PKI) in achieving fault tolerance.
topic_summary: The lecture covers the Tendermint protocol, emphasizing the PKI assumption and its role in consensus mechanisms. It explains the concept of rounds in the protocol, how nodes agree on time, and the differences in block heights during asynchronous phases. The importance of shared global clocks and message delays is also highlighted.
